pr.title: Add `pre-commit` to `reqs/dev.in` and update documentation, then format code
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added pre-commit as a development dependency, scoped the clang-format pre-commit hook to C/C++ source/header files under src, updated contributing documentation with pre-commit setup commands, regenerated reqs/dev.txt, and applied clang-format-driven formatting across a large set of existing source and header files. The PR resolved issue #1543 and was primarily repository tooling plus mechanical formatting cleanup rather than semantic decompilation work.
what_changed: Regenerated `reqs/dev.txt`, adding pre-commit and its dependencies such as cfgv, identify, nodeenv, pyyaml, virtualenv, distlib, and filelock; the generated header changed from Python 3.11 / `pip-compile --strip-extras reqs/dev.in` to Python 3.12 / `pip-compile --output-file=reqs/dev.txt reqs/dev.in`.
Updated `.pre-commit-config.yaml` so the clang-format hook only runs on files matching `^src/.*\.(c|h|cpp)$`.
Updated `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` to recommend installing dev requirements, installing the pre-commit hook, and running `pre-commit run --all-files` for existing code.
Applied broad clang-format cleanup across 92 files, with large mechanical changes in menu, stage, game-mode, effect, fighter, item, and library files.

Formatting changes included line wrapping long calls and prototypes, adjusting indentation, removing extra blank lines between includes, and reflowing complex expressions without evidence of intended behavioral changes.

One visible include cleanup removed `#include "ef/efdata.h"` from `src/melee/db/dballoc.c`; the provided context does not prove whether this was purely formatter-adjacent or an independently necessary unused-include cleanup.
smart_moves: Scoped the pre-commit clang-format hook to `src` C/C++ files, avoiding accidental formatting of non-source files or tooling files outside the intended area.
Bundled the dependency addition with documentation, making the expected contributor workflow explicit instead of only changing config.
Ran the formatter over existing files after enabling the hook, reducing the chance that future contributors hit unrelated formatting diffs on their first commit.
Used generated requirements output rather than only editing `reqs/dev.in`, keeping pinned development dependencies reproducible.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation repositories, introduce formatting enforcement with a one-time bulk formatting pass so later matching/naming PRs are not polluted by style-only churn.
Keep formatting-only PRs separate from semantic matching work whenever possible; this PR's huge diff is best treated as mechanical cleanup, not evidence for function behavior or symbol intent.
When reviewing future diffs after this PR, beware that many line-wrapping and indentation changes are formatter fallout and should not be interpreted as decomp progress.
Document the exact contributor command sequence for hooks; relying on implicit knowledge of `git clang-format` or pre-commit setup creates inconsistent formatting across PRs.
Hook file filters matter: in this repo, restricting clang-format to `src/.*\.(c|h|cpp)` helps preserve intended scope.
naming_conventions: No new decomp naming convention is evidenced by the excerpt.

The contributing documentation retained the existing naming warning: avoid naming a function that is not matched or understood; do not assume prior unnamed/misnamed symbols were understood by someone else.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic is evidenced; the PR is tooling and mechanical clang-format cleanup.
Mechanical formatting can change source layout substantially without implying new matches, new control-flow understanding, or intentional register/stack matching tactics.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided context.
The PR body only states `Resolves #1543.`; no reviewer rationale or requested changes are available in the slice.
follow_up_queries: pre-commit-config.yaml clang-format files src c h cpp
